summary: Advanced matching in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c by adding several plbonuslib functions and exposing enough stale-move/player-bonus struct layout to type their field accesses. The decomp.dev report credited 4 new full matches totaling +896 bytes: pl_8003FAA8, pl_8003EB30, pl_80040948, and fn_8003E998, plus partial improvements for pl_80040688 and pl_800407C8. Supporting header changes corrected prototypes, converted some Vec2 fields to S32Vec2 where integer component access was needed, added an inline HSD_ASSERT helper for the repeated total != 0.0F check, and replaced padding with named stale-move float fields.

key_files.why: Main implementation work: added fn_8003E998, pl_8003EB30, pl_8003FAA8, pl_80040688, pl_800407C8, and pl_80040948, and replaced an open-coded assert with plCheckTotal.
Exposed StaleMoveTable fields xC70, xC74, xC78 and pl_804D6470_t fields xE8/xEC needed by the new plbonuslib logic.
Added inline plCheckTotal(float total) using HSD_ASSERT(0x10, total != 0.0F), preserving the assert source/line behavior for matching.
Changed Struct2074.x207C from Vec2 to S32Vec2, matching integer stale-move assignment to ft_800898A8(gobj)->x207C.y.
Corrected Item xC78/xC80 comments and changed xC80 and xD9C from Vec2 to S32Vec2 to reflect integer-vector usage.
Changed a flexible array member to use UNK_SIZE_ARRAY for project-compatible unknown-size array notation.
Refined pl_80040ED4 prototype to void pl_80040ED4(int, int, int, int, int, float), enabling typed calls from pl_8003EB30.
Refined pl_80038898 prototype to void pl_80038898(int, int), enabling typed calls from pl_80040688.
Adjusted pl_8003EB30 declaration from mixed unsigned/u8 parameters to int parameters matching the implementation.

what_changed: Added full matching implementations for fn_8003E998, pl_8003EB30, pl_8003FAA8, and pl_80040948 according to the decomp.dev report.
Added partial implementations/improvements for pl_80040688 and pl_800407C8; decomp.dev reported 72.50% and 63.64% respectively rather than full matches.
Introduced plCheckTotal in plbonusinline.h and used it in pl_8003ED0C, pl_8003FAA8, pl_800407C8, and pl_80040948 for repeated nonzero-total assertions.
Exposed stale-move table fields xC70, xC74, and xC78 in src/melee/pl/types.h so pl_8003EB30 could update specific float counters instead of indexing through a raw byte blob.
Exposed pl_804D6470_t.xEC, used by pl_80040688 in a gm_8016AEDC() <= pl_804D6470->xEC check.
Corrected several type declarations from Vec2 to S32Vec2 in fighter/item structs, aligning fields with integer vector semantics observed in the new code.
Refined function prototypes for pl_80040ED4, pl_80038898, and pl_8003EB30 from UNK_PARAMS or less precise signatures to concrete parameter lists.
smart_moves: Factored the assert into plCheckTotal in plbonusinline.h, preserving the expected HSD_ASSERT location while avoiding duplicated open-coded __assert blocks.
Used precise field typing, especially S32Vec2 for fields later assigned integer values, instead of forcing casts in function bodies.
Converted only the needed portions of StaleMoveTable padding into named fields, leaving remaining unknown regions as padding and marking xC78 with a TODO size note.
Kept partially matched functions in-tree where useful: pl_80040688 and pl_800407C8 improved substantial byte coverage even without becoming 100% matches.
Refined cross-file prototypes before calling into other pl modules, reducing reliance on UNK_PARAMS and making call sites more informative for future work.
decomp_lessons: Small struct-layout corrections can unlock multiple plbonuslib matches; inspect nearby field accesses before trying to force codegen with local rewrites.
For asserts generated from inline headers, preserving the inline helper and line number can matter for matching; here HSD_ASSERT(0x10, total != 0.0F) was centralized as plCheckTotal.
When a field is accessed as integer components, prefer correcting the struct field type to S32Vec2 over treating it as a float Vec2 with casts.
Expose just enough of large unknown structs to match confirmed accesses, and keep unresolved sizes explicit with TODOs or padding rather than overclaiming layout knowledge.
Bot match reports are useful evidence: this PR demonstrably increased GALE01 matched code by +0.02% and +896 bytes, but only four functions were confirmed as full matches.
naming_conventions: Unknown or not-yet-renamed functions retain address-based names such as pl_8003EB30, pl_8003FAA8, pl_80040688, and fn_8003E998.
Struct fields continue to use offset-style names such as xC70, xC74, xC78, xD6C, xD08, xCEC, and xCF0 when semantic names are not yet known.
Unknown-size arrays use the project macro UNK_SIZE_ARRAY, as applied to itPeachTurnipAttributes.x8.
Inline helper naming uses camel-style semantic prefixing in plbonusinline.h: plCheckTotal.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(0x10) in newly decompiled functions where stack shape needed nudging.
Used an unsigned range idiom, if (arg5 - 0x5C <= 1U), in pl_8003EB30 to match a compact two-value check.

Introduced local temporaries and re-fetches, such as the scoped tmp pointer from Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2(arg1) before setting xD08 = 0, likely to preserve expected register/lifetime behavior.
In pl_800407C8, copied xCEC through a local float tmp before plCheckTotal and division, suggesting expression shape mattered for codegen.
Kept explicit int parameter types in pl_8003EB30 rather than narrower unsigned short/u8 declarations, matching the implementation and call ABI evidence in the diff.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported matched code at 31.99%, +0.02%, +896 bytes, with four new full matches and two partial improvements.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish matching pl_80040688 and pl_800407C8.
Search for future renames or size resolution for StaleMoveTable.xC78, which remained float xC78[1] with a TODO.
Check later struct-layout PRs touching Struct2074.x207C, Item.xC80, or Item.xD9C to see whether S32Vec2 names/semantics were confirmed.
Search plbonusinline.h history for additional inline assert helpers modeled after plCheckTotal.
